title: scurrula reinwardtiana
intro: Scurrula reinwardtiana, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species in the family Santalaceae, which is a type of hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Traditionally, Scurrula reinwardtiana has been used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and acting as an antihistamine. Scurrula reinwardtiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia.
